name: Fortaleza de la Mota y bosque de las esculturas - circular trailrunning por el sendero los Zumaques
region_intro: Jogging around Alcalá La Real offers a chance to explore the region's varied landscape, characterized by rolling hills, olive groves, and historical sites. The area features a network of trails that wind through both natural and cultural landmarks. Runners can expect routes that traverse open countryside and forested sections, providing diverse terrain for different preferences. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making many routes accessible for regular running. Best jogging routes around Alcalá La Real The most popular jogging route is Fortaleza de la Mota y bosque de las esculturas - circular trailrunning por el sendero los Zumaques, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the historic fortress and passes through a sculpture forest. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Salograr, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This trail provides a scenic loop through the local countryside, ideal for a steady run. Local runners also love the Alcalá la Real loop from Alcalá la Real, a 7.4 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through the surrounding rural landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Alcalá La Real is defined by rolling hills, olive groves, and established trails, with options for various ability levels. The network includes both shorter, more accessible paths and longer routes for endurance training. The routes in Alcalá La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 230 runners have used komoot to explore Alcalá La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alcalá La Real?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Alcalá La Real, offering a variety of distances and experiences. The komoot community has explored more than 230 of these trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Alcalá La Real?
A: The running routes in Alcalá La Real feature a diverse landscape of rolling hills, expansive olive groves, and established trails. You'll find paths winding through both open countryside and forested sections, providing varied terrain for different preferences. The elevation changes are generally moderate.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Alcalá La Real?
A: While many routes are classified as moderate, their accessible elevation changes make them suitable for regular runners and those looking to build endurance. For a good starting point, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38140635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Salograr&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path.

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes in Alcalá La Real?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Alcalá La Real are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989205813/fortaleza-de-la-mota-y-bosque-de-las-esculturas-circular-trailrunning-por-el-sendero-los-zumaques" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota y bosque de las esculturas - circular trailrunning por el sendero los Zumaques&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.7-mile (7.6 km) loop.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of running trails in Alcalá La Real?
A: Running trails in the area vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989205813/fortaleza-de-la-mota-y-bosque-de-las-esculturas-circular-trailrunning-por-el-sendero-los-zumaques" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota y bosque de las esculturas&lt;/a&gt; route is about 4.7 miles (7.6 km) and takes approximately 58 minutes. Longer options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá la Real loop&lt;/a&gt; extend to 7.4 miles (11.8 km), typically completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions I can see while jogging?
A: Absolutely! Many routes offer views of historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989205813/fortaleza-de-la-mota-y-bosque-de-las-esculturas-circular-trailrunning-por-el-sendero-los-zumaques" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota y bosque de las esculturas&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, provides views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt; and passes through a unique sculpture forest. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Anthony&lt;/a&gt; on some trails.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass by waterfalls in the Alcalá La Real area?
A: While the immediate jogging routes might not directly feature waterfalls, the broader region around Alcalá La Real is known for its natural beauty, including several waterfalls. You can explore nearby highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chircales Sanctuary and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3155607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Caballo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible via other routes or short excursions.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alcalá La Real?
A: The running trails in Alcalá La Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of olive groves and historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are the running trails in Alcalá La Real dog-friendly?
A: Many outdoor trails in Spain, including those around Alcalá La Real, are generally dog-friendly, especially in rural and natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected areas, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet. Always check local signage for specific restrictions.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Alcalá La Real?
A: Alcalá La Real, located in Andalusia, generally experiences mild winters and hot summers. The most pleasant times for running are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November), when temperatures are moderate. Early mornings or late afternoons are best during the warmer months to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Alcalá La Real Castle?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent views of the iconic castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27626033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alcalá la Real Castle loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase these panoramic vistas, offering a 4.7-mile (7.5 km) run with great photo opportunities.

Q: What is the difficulty level of most running routes in Alcalá La Real?
A: The majority of running routes in Alcalá La Real are classified as moderate. This means they typically involve some elevation changes and varied terrain but are generally well-maintained and accessible for those with a reasonable level of fitness. There are no routes specifically listed as 'easy' or 'difficult' in the immediate selection, indicating a consistent moderate challenge.
tips: Also known as the castle of Alcalá la Real, the fortress of La Mota is a defensive enclosure, located on the hill of La Mota at 1029m, in the city of Alcalá la Real, in the province of Jaén. It is dated from the Nasrid period (13th and 14th centuries), although with some previous elements.

It is a small forest with a few sculptures carved in the natural rock of the area. At the top, there is a cave with a statue of a goat "watching over" the city.
To access it (it is not signposted) you have to take a small path between a small booth with the Nativity of San Marcos and a stone wall.

waypoints.name: Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)
